pi_items.source: setup_geoserver.sh creates 'ne' workspace; setup_task.sh configures it; verified against task scripts
export_result.sh line 79 defines expected WMS Service Title; verifier.py checks for exact match in WMS Capabilities XML

OGC standards (Web Feature Service and Web Coverage Service are real OGC services); GeoServer documentation confirms both are implemented; export_result.sh checks both services; verifier.py scores WFS disabled at 30pts and WCS disabled at 10pts

WMS (Web Map Service) is a real OGC service; GeoServer documentation confirms WMS support at workspace level; export_result.sh checks WMS status and parses WMS Capabilities title; verifier.py scores WMS accessibility at 30pts

pi_items.verified_value: Natural Earth public domain dataset (naturalearthdata.com); shapefiles: ne_110m_admin_0_countries, ne_110m_populated_places, ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines, ne_110m_lakes; imported via ogr2ogr into PostGIS
pi_items.source: setup_geoserver.sh lines 85-122 import Natural Earth shapefiles; Natural Earth project website confirms public domain status; shapefiles verified as real datasets from naturalearthdata.com

privileged_info_summary: The task configures workspace-specific OGC services in GeoServer. The 'ne' workspace is populated with real Natural Earth public domain geographic data (countries, populated places, rivers, and lakes at 110m scale). The task requires disabling WFS and WCS services while keeping WMS enabled, and setting the WMS Service Title to 'Natural Earth Visualization Service'. All three services (WFS, WCS, WMS) are standard OGC implementations in GeoServer. The workspace-level service configuration method is a standard GeoServer feature that allows virtual services isolated to specific workspaces.
